item.description:
# WoWSilicon 2.5.1
## WoWSilicon
- Checks if game path is actually a client
## Dependencies
- Added a Dependencies tab in the options. - Added one-click setup for the Microsoft Visual C++ 2022 runtime. - The runtime is installed into the shared Wine prefix using the patched CrossOver binaries. - Added Git detection for the addon manager. - Added a Git install prompt when Git is missing and users open or use the addon manager.
## Addons
- Addon installs and updates now fail more clearly when Git is missing. - Users can install Git from WoWSilicon instead of having addon actions silently fail.
## Telemetry
- Updated anonymous telemetry to support monthly public stats.
As always, drag it into your Applications directory and run:
```sh xattr -cr /Applications/WoWSilicon.app ```
Supported versions: macOS 15, macOS 26

# WoWSilicon 2.5.0 is here. A new path.
We have a new name, a new launcher, and the project is once again open source.
## WoWSilicon
- You can now set up your own profiles for 1.12.1, 2.4.3, or 3.3.5a based clients. - Allows the launch of third-party launchers. - Installed addons are now renamed automatically. - Addon lists can now be exported. - Reworked the graphics settings section. - Added a realmlist tab in the options. - The launcher can now auto-update again. - Added opt-in telemetry for stats tracking. - Added a force quit button.
## CrossOver
- Ships with a custom `ntdll.so` which includes the Tahoe patch and `rosetta_x87_path` environment variable. - Dropped CrossOver 25 support.
## New Game Patching Method
- All game versions are sharing the same patching method, which no longer modifies the game binary. - Game validation will work properly again. - Ascension can now be launched.
## libSiliconPatch for WrathSilicon
- Added 100 new function hooks which accelerate 3D performance. - Overall, it delivers around 2x more FPS in crowded areas.
## d9vk
- Fixed a pipeline issue which caused cursor flickering.
## rosettax87_JIT
- Updated to the latest version. - macOS 26.5 can be used.
Delete your old `TurtleSilicon.app` and replace it with this one.
As always, drag it into your Applications directory and run:
```sh xattr -cr /Applications/WoWSilicon.app ```
Supported versions: macOS 15, macOS 26
